sections.text: Uses connected E*TRADE holdings excluding cash and money market. Technical engine uses daily market data, EMA/SMA/MACD/RS, and portfolio tolerance controls.

sections.title: Macro Risk Dashboard
rows: Gold/defense matter more if risk shock rises.
Affects XLE, inflation expectations, and consumer pressure.
Controls how aggressive adds should be.
Affects growth stocks, banks, real estate, and bond sleeves.
Affects ADRs, gold, and valuation multiples.
Confirms whether rotation supports risk assets.

sections.title: Macro Summary
sections.text: Macro reads use market proxies for oil, yields/bonds, dollar, gold, high-yield credit, and SPY trend. Headlines, Fed calendar, CPI/jobs, and earnings still need live review.

sections.title: Sector Summary
sections.text: Leadership requires both trend confirmation and relative strength versus SPY. Mixed or lagging sectors are not add candidates without a separate macro catalyst.

sections.title: Prior Week Recap + Forward Trend
sections.text: Forward trend: SPY is Needs data with 20-day change of 0.00%. Confirmed technical leadership: none. Weak/lagging sectors: none. Use live macro/news and earnings checks before trading.

sections.title: Total Suggested Trims and Primary Goal
rows: Keep actions small, reduce overlap, protect profit outliers, and add only when macro/sector thesis confirms technical trend.

sections.title: Aggressive Growth Setup With Risk Controls
rows: SPY/XLK above 20 EMA and 50 SMA with MACD > signal
Starter size only; do not add into weak macro
Large P&L outlier or overweight sleeve
Safety sleeve overweight and growth leadership confirmed
Below 20 EMA/50 SMA with MACD < signal
No averaging down without macro confirmation

sections.title: Key Catalysts to Watch
rows: Affects growth multiples, banks, real estate, and fixed income.
Affects energy, inflation pressure, defense, and gold.
Important for CLOZ, banks, and risk appetite.
SPY breadth and sector relative strength
Confirms whether rotation is broadening or narrowing.
